generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance RobotDriveController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller for closed-loop control of industrial robot axes"
]

instance PMSMActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving robot axes with cycloidal gear transmissions for precise torque control"
]

instance FrictionCompensationController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Real-time adaptive controller for friction compensation in machining processes"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FrictionMetricsData
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance FirstAxisEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder for the first robot axis measuring angular position and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EncoderPositionData
]

instance SecondAxisEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder for the second robot axis measuring angular position and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EncoderPositionDataSecondAxis
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Input current sensor measuring electrical parameters for motor control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring temperature, humidity, and other operational factors"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParameters
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance EncoderPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisEncoder
]

instance EncoderPositionDataSecondAxis : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondAxisEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalParameters : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance FrictionMetricsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based friction model (white-box) for joint friction compensation using Simulink simulations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnvironment
]

instance DataDrivenFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven friction model (black-box) using machine learning for nonlinear relationship capture"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NeuralNetworkTraining
]

instance FrictionCompensationSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based dynamic simulation of friction compensation during machining processes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnvironment, FrictionModel
]

instance ReferenceFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UML class diagram reference model for friction shadow concepts"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLReferenceModel
]

instance FrictionTraceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces of friction characteristics from robot operation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderPositionData, MotorCurrentData, EnvironmentalParameters
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModel, DataDrivenFrictionModel
]

instance OperationalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data traces including friction characteristics, loads, and environmental factors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionMetricsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionCompensationController
]

instance ProcessParameterData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data for machining process parameters (cutting speeds, feed rates) used for optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalParameters
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveOptimization
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System Modeling Language (SysML) framework for defining requirements, functional architecture, and logical architecture of the Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReferenceFrictionModel
]

instance SimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for executable friction models and real-time simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModel, FrictionCompensationSimulation
]

instance NeuralNetworkTraining : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data-driven processing components for training machine learning models from contextual data traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataDrivenFrictionModel
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time data transmission from robot sensors to Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables EncoderPositionData, EncoderPositionDataSecondAxis, MotorCurrentData, EnvironmentalParameters
]

instance ThingsBoardIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) integration with ThingsBoard IoT platform for standardized data exchange"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionTraceData, OperationalDataTrace
]

instance DataPreprocessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dedicated preprocessing component for filtering raw sensor data (low-pass filtering) before MQTT transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables EncoderPositionData, EncoderPositionDataSecondAxis, MotorCurrentData, EnvironmentalParameters
]

instance ModelBasedSystemsEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE methodologies (RFLP, OOSEM) for multi-layered abstraction from requirements to behavior modeling"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReferenceFrictionModel, FrictionModel
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Customized Digital Shadow Reference Model for robot-specific friction compensation use case"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionTraceData, OperationalDataTrace
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionCompensation : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters to mitigate friction-induced positioning errors in robotic machining"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance PhysicsDataDrivenModelIntegration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous updating of friction models (white-box/black-box) using streaming data traces from physical twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelUpdateInsight
]

instance PredictiveOptimization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulative parameter tuning for machining processes (cutting speeds, feed rates) to reduce CO2 emissions or improve quality"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight
]

instance ProactiveFaultDetection : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring friction anomalies (wear, temperature drift) for predictive maintenance in robotics"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of control parameters to compensate for friction-induced errors in robot positioning"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelUpdateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time analysis of model relevance across varying operational conditions (loads, environmental factors)"
]

instance ProcessOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized machining parameters derived from digital twin simulations for improved process efficiency"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of friction anomalies (wear, temperature drift) enabling predictive maintenance actions"
]

instance ParameterTuningRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendation of cutting speed/feed rate adjustments based on friction compensation analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered when friction anomalies exceed predefined thresholds, initiating predictive maintenance procedures"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance RobotMachiningEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RobotDriveController, PMSMActuator, FirstAxisEncoder, SecondAxisEncoder, CurrentSensor, EnvironmentalSensor,
                 FrictionModel, DataDrivenFrictionModel, FrictionCompensationController
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates in real-time, leveraging live data streams for continuous synchronization between the physical robot and its digital counterpart. The synchronization is designed to support dynamic adaptation of physics-based (white-box) and data-driven (black-box) friction models, ensuring real-time accuracy. The system emphasizes high-precision applications where latency requirements are critical for maintaining positioning accuracy and process quality. While the documents do not specify exact latency thresholds, the emphasis is on real-time processing and immediate model updates to compensate for wear, temperature variations, and payload changes during runtime. The pipeline integrates live data from the robot’s joints and machining processes, enabling autonomous friction identification and model refinement in near-real-time conditions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is structured using **SysML structural models** to describe the robot’s architecture and **Simulink behavioral models** for simulating friction dynamics. These models are integrated into an **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework, enabling traceability of data and computation methods. The orchestration involves a **centralized pipeline** that automatically populates the digital shadow from live robot data, including real-time friction simulations, via a **UML-based sequence diagram** (e.g., AAS Executor). The system does not mention hybrid orchestration, decentralized execution, or FMI integration beyond the AAS context."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Lifecycle Phases of a Digital Twin System:**
  The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system in the provided context is structured around **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, particularly those aligned with **Digital Shadows** and **concretization** of reference models. The lifecycle is segmented into the following phases, with transitions managed via **conformance relations** and **traceability** between models:

  1. **Requirements & Conceptualization Phase**
     - Defined through **requirements analysis** and **purpose-driven modeling** (e.g., for manufacturing processes like injection molding).
     - Uses **SysML** or **ADLs** (e.g., AADL, MontiArc) to capture functional and logical architecture, with **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** or **OOSEM** methodologies for structuring complexity.
     - Digital Shadows are initially designed based on **data traces** from the physical system (e.g., sensor inputs) or **purpose-driven requirements** (e.g., optimization, control).

  2. **Modeling & Concretization Phase**
     - **Reference models** (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model) are instantiated into **concrete models** via **conformance relations** (e.g., mapping reference classes to domain-specific incarnations).
     - **Processing components** (e.g., simulation models in **Simulink**, discrete-event simulation tools like **DEVS**) are integrated to compute higher-level abstractions from raw data traces.
     - **Metadata** and **traceability** (via associations) ensure alignment between physical and digital representations, enabling **transitive traceability** (e.g., linking input traces to processing components).

  3. **Simulation & Validation Phase**
     - **Simulation tools** (e.g., **Simulink**, discrete-event simulators) validate the DT’s behavior against engineering models (e.g., product/technical layer models).
     - **State machines** (in SysML v1.7) or **MontiArc Automaton** are used to model system behavior, ensuring compliance with functional and logical architectures.
     - **Version control** (implicitly referenced in **model-driven engineering** frameworks) manages changes across heterogeneous stakeholders’ perspectives.

  4. **Operational & Evolution Phase**
     - The DT operates as a **Digital Shadow** (one-way data flow) or **active Digital Twin** (bidirectional interaction), with governance via **reference model conformance**.
     - Evolution is managed through **purpose-driven updates** (e.g., adapting to new requirements like production planning in injection molding) or **automated processing logic** (e.g., data calculation models).
     - **Lifecycle governance** relies on **conformance relations** to ensure continuity between phases, with **traceability** preserving integrity across model transformations.

  **Key Enablers:**
  - **SysML/ADLs** for structural modeling.
  - **Simulink/DEVS** for behavior simulation.
  - **Traceability frameworks** (e.g., originating from associations) for validation.
  - **Reference model concretization** for adaptability.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The fidelity and validity of the Digital Twin system are ensured through:
1. **Model Integration**: Combination of **white-box (physics-based)** and **black-box (data-driven)** models (e.g., Simulink simulations + machine learning) to capture nonlinear phenomena (e.g., robot friction).
2. **Traceability and Verification**: Methods for ensuring correctness of transformations (e.g., from SysML to simulation models) and maintaining data provenance via origin-to-association mappings.
3. **Dynamic Adaptation**: Continuous updates to models (e.g., retraining neural networks) based on real-time data to compensate for wear, temperature changes, or payload variations.
4. **Validation Methods**: Focus on **real-time requirements** (e.g., control system dead times) and **simulative experiments** (e.g., compensating for external machining forces) to validate model accuracy before deployment.
**Not explicitly mentioned** probabilistic latent variable models or detailed error detection/correction mechanisms beyond traceability and model integration approaches."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on the **MQTT protocol** for communication, enabling a publish-subscribe architecture where data flows are hierarchical and organized via MQTT topics. The system employs the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as the single source of truth for data representation, integrating SysML models (for structural traceability) and FMU units (for simulation-based computations).

  The network infrastructure is not explicitly detailed, but the system leverages **standardized IoT communication protocols** (MQTT) and **real-time data processing pipelines** (e.g., preprocessing, MQTT broker, AAS executor). Security protocols (e.g., TLS) are **not mentioned**, though the hierarchical topic-based access model facilitates selective data access. The architecture prioritizes **reliability** through automated data aggregation and traceability mechanisms tied to the AAS and SysML models.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment** infrastructure is not explicitly detailed in the documents regarding cloud/edge/on-premise deployment. However, the system integrates:
  - **SysML models** for structural representation of the robot’s components.
  - **Simulation models** (FMU units from Simulink) for behavioral modeling of friction dynamics.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as the central data model, enabling standardized submodel-based representation of assets (e.g., robots, sensors) throughout their lifecycle.

  The deployment relies on **ThingsBoard** (an open-source IoT platform) for **real-time data visualization and dashboarding**, where users interact with the digital shadow’s data. The pipeline includes:
  - **MQTT broker** for data routing.
  - **AAS executor** for interpreting AAS models and constructing the digital shadow.
  - **Preprocessing components** for noise filtering and data normalization.

  The system emphasizes **traceability** by linking data traces to their computational origins (SysML/Simulink models) and physical components via the AAS. No explicit scalability or availability metrics are provided, but the architecture supports **automated, real-time updates** of the digital shadow based on live sensor data.
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages **MQTT** for data streaming between the physical robot and its digital twin, ensuring real-time communication. The integration adheres to **traceability principles** by linking data traces back to their computational origins (e.g., processing components, input traces). The system does not explicitly reference ThingsBoard, FMI, AADL, MontiArc, or enterprise systems like ERP/MES. The provided documents emphasize **Industry 4.0 interoperability** via the AAS standard, but no additional external dependencies are detailed."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The provided documents do not explicitly detail **data ownership policies**, **privacy protection measures**, **compliance with data protection regulations**, **consent management**, or **data sharing agreements** for Digital Twin systems. However, the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** in Document 5 outlines foundational concepts where **metadata** and **System Properties** (e.g., time-stamped configurations) are explicitly managed to ensure traceability and contextual integrity. While no explicit governance framework is described, the model’s **purpose-driven design** implies that:
- **Data traces** are tied to specific **assets** and **purposes**, enforcing controlled access via **conformance relations** (e.g., mapping between reference and concrete models).
- **Engineering models** (e.g., UML-based) may implicitly support compliance by documenting system configurations, though no direct mention of anonymization or regulatory adherence (e.g., GDPR) is present.
Thus, while **privacy and ownership** are not explicitly addressed, the **DSRM’s metadata and traceability mechanisms** provide a structured foundation for future governance frameworks to integrate compliance requirements.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry standards** and **internal reference models**:

1. **Industry Standards**:
   - **Modeling Languages**: **SysML (Interaction Block Diagrams, State Machines)**, **AADL**, **MontiArc Automaton**.
   - **Communication Protocols**: **MQTT** (for data flow between physical and digital entities).
   - **Simulation Tools**: **MathWorks Simulink** (for behavior validation).

2. **Internal Standardization**:
   - **Digital Shadow Reference Model** defines core concepts (e.g., data traces, processing components) with **conformance relations** ensuring model compliance.
   - **Concretization** of the reference model via **class diagrams** (e.g., mapping reference classes to concrete implementations).
   - **Traceability frameworks** (e.g., associations originating from data traces) enforce consistency across models.

3. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - **No external interoperability standards** (e.g., ThingsBoard, Eclipse Layout Kernel) are referenced; focus is on **MBSE-driven standardization**.
   - **Certification requirements** are implied via **conformance relations** to the Digital Shadow Reference Model.
"
]
